rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEVEN E. LITTMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Administrator'S Motion To Appoint Receiver
rulings.Rulings: The motion to appoint a receiver is denied. It is doubtful that the Probate Court has jurisdiction given that the property over which a receivership is sought is not property of the decedent¿s estate, which only has an investment interest in that property. In any event, there is already a pending civil action concerning this property, which has been ordered to arbitration. Even if the matter were properly before this Court, the motion is procedurally improper because it seeks ultimate relief. It would need to be brought either as a motion for preliminary equitable relief conforming to the procedural requirements of a motion for preliminary injunction or as a motion for summary judgment conforming to those procedural requirements. In any event, even if the motion were procedurally proper, it would be denied because the Administrator has not carried her burden of showing the necessity of such extreme relief. Any party who wishes to argue the tentative ruling/motion must advise opposing parties and notify the Courtroom Clerk (415-551-3702) by 5:00 p.m. on Tuesday, June 5, 2012.
